sections.paragraph:
ϰόλπος, ου, ὁ [etym. complex]

—1. ‘front of the (human) chest’, chest, bosom, breast, in NT always in ref. to special social recognition and status through personal contact Lk 16:22f; J 1:18; 13:23.

—2. of the fold of a garment beginning at the chest and forming a kind of pocket fold, lap Lk 6:38.

—3. of a portion of a body of water on a shoreline bay Ac 27:39.
